perturbation.original_text: A. Batch Processing. M&I will initiate batch processing and have bank operations reports available for transmission to Customer or make the processed item and reports available, within five (5) hours on all (but two) processing days in a calendar month [fifteen (15) hours at year end] provided M&I receives all input data from Customer at the Operations Center by 1:00 a.m. (local time of the Operations Center).
perturbation.changed_text: A. Batch Processing. M&I will use reasonable efforts to initiate batch processing and have bank operations reports available for transmission to Customer or make the processed item and reports available, within eight (8) hours on all processing days in a calendar month [twenty (20) hours at year end] provided M&I receives all input data from Customer at the Operations Center by 3:00 a.m. (local time of the Operations Center).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by changing the definite 'will initiate' to the more flexible 'will use reasonable efforts to initiate'. Furthermore the timeframe for having bank operations reports available have been extended. This weakens the performance standards outlined. This change contradicts Section 9.1 where M&I agrees to perform the Services in accordance with the Performance Standards, now these standards are weakened.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. PERFORMANCE STANDARDS 9.1 General. Except as otherwise specified in this Agreement, M&I agrees to perform the Services in accordance with the Performance Standards and, where there are no Performance Standards, in a commercially reasonable manner and with no other or higher degree of care. M&I's performance under this Agreement shall be excused to the extent any delays are caused by the occurrence of an event of force majeure.
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a strong in-text contradiction. The original text stipulated specific performance standards for batch processing, including definite timelines ('will initiate' within five hours). The changed text weakens these standards by introducing 'reasonable efforts' and extending the timelines to eight hours. This contradicts Section 9.1, which mandates M&I to perform the Services in accordance with the Performance Standards. The modification introduces ambiguity and provides M&I with more leeway, thus creating a direct conflict with the commitment to adhere to strict Performance Standards as outlined in Section 9.1, which now no longer aligns with the changed section, resulting in an in-text contradiction under the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The sessions shall be held at an M&I Datacenter location to be determined by M&I. Customer shall be responsible for all Expenses incurred by the participants in connection with such education and training.
perturbation.changed_text: The sessions shall be held at a location to be mutually determined between M&I and the Customer. Customer shall only be responsible for 50% of the Expenses incurred by the participants in connection with such education and training.
perturbation.explanation: This modification changes the location determination from solely M&I's discretion to a mutual agreement, and it also reduces the customer's financial responsibility for training expenses to 50%. This introduces a contradiction with section 8.11, where it's mentioned that workshop fees and training fees aren't excluded costs, implying that customer should be responsible for it fully.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.11 Excluded Costs. The fees set forth in the Fee Schedule do not include communication costs, telecommunication charges, printline charges and other output costs, Expenses, third party pass-thru charges, workshop fees, training fees and late fees or charges and Taxes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Customer is responsible for all expenses, while the changed text reduces the customer's responsibility to 50%. This directly contradicts section 8.11, which lists training fees as excluded costs from the fee schedule, implicitly suggesting that the customer is fully responsible for them, creating a clear inconsistency regarding the financial obligations of the customer.

perturbation.original_text: A. If M&I terminates this Agreement following an Event of Default on the part of Customer, or if Customer terminates this Agreement in accordance with Section 11.1 above without complying with the notification requirements set forth in Section 11.1, then Customer shall pay M&I a termination fee ("Termination for Cause Fee") in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value, payable as set forth in Section 11.1 above. In addition to the foregoing, Customer shall pay to M&I, reasonable Expenses in connection with the disposition of equipment, facilities and contracts related to M&I's performance of the Services on behalf of Customer. The Termination for Cause Fee shall not be subject to the limitations set forth in Section 13.4.
perturbation.changed_text: A. If M&I terminates this Agreement following an Event of Default on the part of Customer, or if Customer terminates this Agreement in accordance with Section 11.1 above without complying with the notification requirements set forth in Section 11.1, then Customer shall pay M&I a termination fee ("Termination for Cause Fee") in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value, payable as set forth in Section 11.1 above. In addition to the foregoing, Customer shall pay to M&I, reasonable Expenses in connection with the disposition of equipment, facilities and contracts related to M&I's performance of the Services on behalf of Customer. The Termination for Cause Fee **SHALL** be subject to the limitations set forth in Section 13.4.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Termination for Cause Fee SHALL be subject to the limitations set forth in Section 13.4, whereas the original text explicitly stated that it shall not be. This creates an in-text contradiction as it directly opposes a previous statement about the applicability of the liability limitations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. If M&I terminates this Agreement following an Event of Default on the part of Customer, or if Customer terminates this Agreement in accordance with Section 11.1 above without complying with the notification requirements set forth in Section 11.1, then Customer shall pay M&I a termination fee ("Termination for Cause Fee") in an amount equal to REDACTED of the Estimated Remaining Value, payable as set forth in Section 11.1 above. In addition to the foregoing, Customer shall pay to M&I, reasonable Expenses in connection with the disposition of equipment, facilities and contracts related to M&I's performance of the Services on behalf of Customer. The Termination for Cause Fee SHALL be subject to the limitations set forth in Section 13.4.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the 'Termination for Cause Fee' is NOT subject to limitations set forth in Section 13.4. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating the 'Termination for Cause Fee' SHALL be subject to those same limitations. This creates a clear and irreconcilable in-text contradiction regarding the applicability of Section 13.4 to the Termination for Cause Fee, which falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.
